workflow_value_hypothesis: AI-assisted support work may be associated with faster case resolution, lower escalation, and improved knowledge reuse.

work_change_evidence.observed_pattern: Search and Assistant activity are present in the support workflow slice.
work_change_evidence.interpretation: Indicates AI-assisted knowledge access is observable.

work_change_evidence.observed_pattern: Skills or agent runs are present in aggregate support work.
work_change_evidence.interpretation: Indicates reusable or delegated support workflows may be emerging.
Supports quality and trust review, but does not prove correctness.

work_change_evidence.observed_pattern: Recovery and abandonment signals are visible.
work_change_evidence.interpretation: Provides caveats around friction and workflow reliability.

outcome_signal_recommendations.interpretation: Tests whether aggregate support case resolution time moved in the expected direction.
Tests whether fewer cases required escalation in the comparison window.
Tests whether fewer resolved cases reopened after AI-assisted support work.
Provides directional context for whether support backlog moved with the workflow change.

claim_confidence.reason: AI work evidence and customer-owned support outcome signals align directionally, but causality and realized ROI are not established.

claim_states.reason: Aggregate AI work evidence and support outcome context support a bounded value-investigation claim.
Capacity movement is directionally aligned but remains associational and assumption-bound.
The packet does not prove ROI or causality.

safe_claims.claim: Aggregate support evidence suggests this workflow is a candidate for value investigation.
safe_claims.required_caveat: Directionally aligned evidence does not prove ROI or causality.

safe_claims.claim: Observed AI work patterns align with a capacity-creation hypothesis for support case resolution.
safe_claims.required_caveat: Customer-owned outcome data must remain attached to the same aggregate slice.

safe_claims.claim: Support workflow data can test whether AI-assisted work is associated with lower reopen or escalation rates.
safe_claims.required_caveat: Case mix, staffing, channel mix, and process changes may explain movement.

blocked_claims.claim: Glean proved ROI for the support organization.
blocked_claims.reason: Outcome movement and AI work evidence do not establish realized ROI.
The MVP preserves NOT_CAUSAL posture unless separately governed.

blocked_claims.claim: Named employees or creators saved a specific number of hours.
One team or manager group performs better with AI.
blocked_claims.reason: Comparative team or manager ranking is outside the governance boundary.

required_caveats: This is a caveated value evidence pack, not ROI proof.
Outcome movement is associational and must not be described as causal.
All interpretation is aggregate-only and scoped to the approved support workflow slice.

next_actions.action: Review the support outcome export with the business owner and confirm the same workflow slice, baseline, and comparison windows.
Decide whether capacity, quality, cost, or experience should be the primary pilot story.

executive_summary: Customer Support is a caveated AI Value Intelligence pilot candidate: aggregate AI work evidence is present, support outcome metrics moved in the expected direction, and claim language must remain bounded to value investigation rather than ROI proof.
